signals.reasoning: BTC is still stabilizing after the flush, but the board remains under a live CAUTION regime tied to oil and geopolitics. That is enough uncertainty to avoid forcing a fresh directional bet here.
ETH has mostly neutralized into post-flush chop. With macro still RED and the event brake still on, there is not enough edge to justify a fresh long or short.
SOL has some residual squeeze fuel from negative funding and prior liquidation behavior, but the chart is still just a damaged stabilization range. It stays watch-only rather than actionable.

STABLE has one of the strongest squeeze setups on the sheet with deeply negative funding and still-positive momentum, and CAUTION is lighter than a full veto. This is still a reduced-size long only, because the move is already underway and headline risk remains real.
GOAT is already far into a liquidation-style flush, which makes fresh shorts late and fresh longs too unstable. There is no clean new entry here.
DOT has strong negative funding on paper, but the chart is still damaged and the move lower is already mature enough to make timing awkward. I would watch for a cleaner reclaim before upgrading it.
BLUR has squeeze fuel, but the structure still looks like late post-flush stabilization rather than a high-quality reversal. Under CAUTION, it does not quite clear the bar for a fresh signal.
AAVE has better liquidity than most of the alts here, but the move already looks extended relative to its daily range and does not have strong funding support behind it. That leaves it as watch-only.

IMX is an acceptable reduced-size squeeze long because funding is still meaningfully negative while price has only partially recovered. It is not a high-conviction trade, but it is one of the cleaner upside setups if the market keeps squeezing crowded shorts.

0G remains the strongest raw squeeze setup on the board with very negative funding and continued upside pressure. Confidence stays capped because it is extended and thin, but under CAUTION rather than VETO it is still one of the few names that merits a small-size long lean.
MON already went through a meaningful damage phase and now looks more like messy stabilization than a clean continuation short. That makes it a poor fresh entry despite the weak daily structure.
